extra_model_fields.notes_for_worker: When adding unit costs, use only values justified by the packet or clearly labeled assumptions. If unit-cost values are not in the packet, state them as assumptions and keep them conservative; prefer cost-per-record semantics to enable clearer aggregation.

planned_changes: Add a dedicated 'Remediation Cost Accounting' section to field_followup.md that lists for each option (O1/O2/O3): unit cost (USD), basis (per-record, per-device, etc.), estimated quantity or scope (per targeted record), cost per record, and aggregated cost across the recommended set. Show final total and verify it is <= remediation_budget_usd (25000).

Select and document a smallest-sufficient remediation plan: specify which records (by record_id) receive which option, deliver rationale (governing driver, risk reduction expected), and show the cost/benefit tradeoff demonstrating minimal sufficient coverage.

Add cost columns to methane_plume_filter.csv (e.g., recommended_option, unit_cost_usd, quantity, cost_usd) and include a final aggregation row (TOTAL_REMEDIATION_COST). Ensure CSV remains machine-parseable.

Include a short deterministic recomputation appendix: show sample recomputations for at least two rows confirming enhancement_norm, wind_norm, cloud_norm, contribution math, and post-O# calculations. Optionally include a small script snippet or checksums so verifiers can re-run numeric checks.

Ensure the full field_followup.md is under artifact size/format limits so previews are not truncated. If necessary split long supporting material into an additional artifact (e.g., remediation_costs.md or remediation_costs.csv) and reference it in the memo.

Repair any submission metadata/serialization issues: avoid duplicated/conflicting 'passed' or 'final_score' fields in grader-visible metadata. Validate artifact visibility after re-submission so verifiers see non-truncated previews.
Re-run the submission and confirm deterministic precheck and model grader see the same, unambiguous artifacts; request re-evaluation.
